title: Reviewer-handoff template + COI/independence + sign-off recording
context: Reviewer sign-off must be recorded in PRs with independence and COI declarations. A standardised handoff template ensures that (a) the drafter does not self-review, (b) the reviewer's qualifications and COI are declared, (c) the full checklist is attached, and (d) the sign-off is persisted in review.yaml for audit. This task depends on the reviewer checklist (review-001).
objective: Create a reviewer-handoff template that includes: the task/object/description/language being reviewed; reviewer name and qualification statement (domain-aware or qualified bilingual); COI declaration (reviewer is independent of the drafting step); completed reviewer checklist reference (review-001); and the sign-off block (approved / approved-with-changes / rejected) with the reviewer's reasoning, which is pasted into the PR description and persisted in review.yaml.
acceptanceCriteria: Template includes: task/object/description/language identifier, reviewer name and qualification role (domain-aware reviewer or qualified bilingual reviewer)
Includes an explicit COI/independence declaration: 'I am not the drafter of this description/translation and have no undisclosed conflict of interest'
Includes a completed-checklist confirmation reference (review-001 checklist) with a field for any noted issues
Includes a sign-off block with three options: APPROVED, APPROVED WITH CHANGES (list required changes), or REJECTED (state reason); all options require a brief reasoning statement
Specifies that the completed handoff is pasted into the PR description and the sign-off outcome is recorded in review.yaml under the appropriate object/description/translation entry
Is consistent with the sign-off recording pattern described in PLAN.md §8 and can be used for both source-description reviews and translation reviews
output: templates/reviewer-handoff.md — reviewer-handoff template for domain-aware and bilingual reviewer sign-off
